title: CRISPR Therapeutics AG Financial Statements and Historical Trends 2014-2026
text: CRISPR Therapeutics Historical Financial Analysis. Revenue Trend Analysis: CRISPR has historically operated at net loss but with volatile revenue patterns driven by milestone payments and collaboration revenues. 2023 revenues of $371 million were inflated by $200 million Vertex milestone payment. Core CASGEVY commercial revenue (2024-2026) represents the emerging baseline revenue generation. Operating Expense Trends: R&D expenses have historically ranged from $70-100 million annually, supporting the company's robust clinical pipeline. General and administrative expenses have remained relatively stable at $15-20 million per quarter. Net Loss Trajectory: Annual net losses have widened to $350+ million levels in recent years as the company accelerates clinical programs. However, profitability is not expected in near-term as investments in pipeline programs continue. Cash Burn Rate: The company's quarterly cash burn has been approximately $30-40 million, with $1.9 billion in cash providing runway through clinical milestones. Cash Runway: Based on current cash position and burn rates, the company has sufficient funding for 4-5 years of clinical operations without additional financing. Balance Sheet Strength: Total assets exceed $2 billion, primarily composed of cash, with minimal debt. The company is in a strong financial position to pursue long-term clinical development strategy. Valuation Multiples: Historical stock valuations have ranged based on pipeline perceived value and commercial traction of CASGEVY.
